entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species being studied for drought adaptation and water use efficiency.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which water is lost from the plant through transpiration.

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The difference between the amount of moisture the air can hold and how much it currently holds, acting as a stressor.

entities.name: SOIL DRYING
entities.description: The progressive reduction of water content in the soil.

entities.name: SOIL WATER CONSERVATION MECHANISMS
entities.description: Physiological processes that help the plant conserve soil water.

entities.name: DROUGHT ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a plant to survive and produce under water-limiting conditions.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINES
entities.description: A population of homozygous lines used for genetic mapping.

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATIONS
entities.description: Groups of plants used to identify the genomic regions associated with specific traits.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE
entities.description: A controlled environment used for conducting plant experiments.

entities.name: GROWTH CHAMBERS
entities.description: Highly controlled environmental systems for plant growth.

entities.name: OUTDOORS
entities.description: Natural field environment used for validating experimental results.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in transpiration rate in response to increasing vapor pressure deficit.

entities.name: TRANSPIRABLE SOIL WATER THRESHOLD
entities.description: The soil moisture fraction at which transpiration begins to decline.

entities.name: EVAPOTRANSPIRATION RESPONSE
entities.description: The combined response of evaporation and transpiration to vapor pressure deficit.

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING FACILITY
entities.description: A facility designed for rapid and automated measurement of plant traits.

entities.name: FIELD DENSITY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Planting density that mimics actual field conditions.

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total area of leaves, normalized with received radiation.

entities.name: PLANT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The physical organization and structure of the plant.

entities.name: PLANT CANOPY ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The spatial arrangement of the plant's canopy.

entities.name: GENETIC MAPPING
entities.description: The process of identifying the location of genes that control specific traits.

entities.name: PLANT WATER USE
entities.description: The overall efficiency and manner in which a plant utilizes water.

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to tolerate drought during the final stages of its growth cycle.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach using genetic markers to select for desired traits.

relationships.description: Sorghum is the crop in which transpiration rate is measured
High vapor pressure deficit limits the transpiration rate
Progressive soil drying limits the transpiration rate
Limiting transpiration rate is a soil water conservation mechanism
Soil water conservation mechanisms play a drought-adaptive role
Recombinant Inbred Lines were developed using sorghum mapping populations
Recombinant Inbred Lines are used as mapping populations
Mapping populations are used to genetically map traits
Vapor pressure deficit triggers the transpiration response
Vapor pressure deficit affects the evapotranspiration response
Plant canopy architecture influences the transpiration response to VPD
Genetic mapping is used to identify traits controlling plant water use
Genetic mapping helps identify components of terminal drought tolerance
Terminal drought tolerance components are incorporated via marker-assisted breeding
